en: Complete a run - your name in the Hall of Fame.
Take the stairs down for the first time.
Throw an item for the first time.
Fire a wand for the first time.
This device magically increases the wielder's accuracy.
You can't be hurt by fire while wearing this.
This device weakens hostile magic before it can affect the wearer.
Magically warded, this device protects the user from physical hurt.
An enchantment on this device causes the wielder to do more damage.
This amulet speeds up attacks and other actions.
This device magically increases the wielder's ability to avoid attacks.
While using this, you have both extra accuracy and extra evasion
This weapon burns foes with magical fire.
This weapon slows foes with magical cold.
This item helps you move more swiftly.
This device renders you immune to poisons.
This armor provides extra protection from weapons, magic, and even fire and poison.
This weapon can help knock foes away from you and into each other.
Sparks crackle from this lightning-infused weapon.
Dripping with toxic fluids, this weapon poisons its wielder's foes.
This device protects you from fire and poison.
This useful item somehow empowers your wands to hit harder.
This weapon strikes with the power of a thunderclap, knocking foes away.
A cruel weapon that tends to hit hard and accurately.
Incoming magic and fire damage is reduced.
Halves magic / fire damage per stack (asymptotic).
*Halves incoming magic and fire damage* - key against wand-users.
Open wound draining health every turn.
Loses HP each turn; bleed-rate tapers down as the stacks count down.
Acting slower than normal due to magical chill.
Move and attack costs rise sharply (more per stack). Combine with wet to freeze.
You're *chilled* - slowed down for a few turns.
Can see creatures even through walls.
Reveals all mobs on the level regardless of line of sight.
Too afraid to approach an enemy; light or magial encouragement might help.
Frightened creatures can't approach danger.  Magical blessing, or moving into the light are the only cures.
You're *frightened* and can't attack terrifying enemies.  The best cure is to walk into *light*.
Frozen in place until incoming damage chips the ice away.
Prevents all motion and action until the buff ends. Taking damage removes 2 stacks
You're *frozen solid* - you can't act until the ice melts or you're hit.
Fly and pass through solid objects like a ghost.
Adds +20 evasion and lets you levitate and pass through walls. Attacking ends the effect.
With this condition you can *fly and pass through walls* -- an excellent way to escape.
Recharging a Haste ability until it can be used again.
Able to move more swiftly than normal.
Move and attack costs reduced by 20% per stack (compounding).
You're *hasted*.  Moves and attacks cost less time - capitalise on it.
Recharging a Heal ability until it can be used again.
Recharging a phase-dodge until it can be used again.
Attempting to hide out of sight of enemies.
Mob remains stationary and cannot be targeted until disturbed.
You're *hiding*.\nEnemies can't see you until you act or the buff wears off.
Filled with hope and courage, less frighened and more effective.
Grants immunity to frightened. Bonus accuracy and evasion scale with stacks.
The whole level layout is revealed.
Marks every tile as explored each turn.
Invisible and hard to notice; attacking cancels the effect.
Adds +40 evasion. Attacking ends the effect.
Evasion bonus and impossible to target -- great for approaching ranged enemies.
Empowered by recent kills to strike and move faster.
Each kill adds stacks; every stack multiplies move and attack costs by 0.9 (compounding) down to a floor. Effect caps at 10 stacks.
Hovering, and unaffected by ground hazards or chasms.
Can cross chasms safely; ignores ground-level surfaces (water, oil).
Smeared with oil, and vulnerable to fire.
Doubles fire damage taken; ignition lasts longer when set on fire.
You're *oiled*. Take *2x damage from fire*.  Step in water to wash it off.
Losing HP each turn until it burns out.
Loses a fixed amount of HP each turn. Doubled if also oily. Ended by being chilled or stepping into water.
You're *burning* - lose health each turn. Water puts it out; oil makes it spread.
Moving at blinding speed and very hard to hit. Ends on taking or dealing damage.
Lets you walk through walls and adds +40 evasion. Attacking or taking damage ends the effect.
A good way to escape or close with foes.  Ends the moment you take or deal damage.
Losing HP each turn until the poison wears off.
Loses HP each turn. Cured by anything that brings Regeneration.
You're *poisoned* - lose health each turn. Armor won't help; heal or wait it out.
*Halves incoming physical damage* (more at higher stacks).
Reloading and preparing to make a ranged attack.
Heals HP each turn while below max HP. Each turn it also strips any active POISONED buff, even at full HP.
You regenerate health each turn while this is active.  Cures poison too.
Completely immune to all damage -- until the effect wears off.
Negates the next instance of incoming damage entirely.
*Immune to all damage* until it wears off.\nUse aggressively while it lasts.
Spells and potions are extra-empowered by sorcery.
Item effects fire at a higher *item* level - more damage, more range, longer durations.
Your *wands* and *potions* work better while this effect is on.
Soaking wet.  Takes extra damage from lightning and cold.
Doubles lightning and quadruples cold damage taken. Combine with chilled to freeze.
You're *wet*. Take extra damage from lightning and cold.

key: # Buff "Effect:" lines (RL-46) - shown in V2BuffInfo; numbers filled by BuffSystem.describeEffectForStacks

en: loses HP each turn (rate tapers as stacks drop)
loses HP each turn from fire
can't be targeted by ranged attacks
takes 2x lightning & 4x cold damage; freezes if chilled
takes 2x fire damage; ignites on contact with fire
flees from the source of fear
ignores surface effects; can cross chasms
can move through walls until next hit
intangible to melee until next hit

key: # DOT-death verb phrases (RL-46) - used by BuffSystem.logDotDeath

en: Expert wand user who fights at a distance using wands and his magical *Shield*.
Bomb specialist who can see through smoke and vegetation and escape with her *Phase* power.
Close combat specialist who closes with enemies and hits hard using his *Charge* ability.
Beacons can *teleport you between floors* once lit -- ideal for backtracking and trying other routes.
Picked up a *magic charge* - it refills wands and items, though some refill faster than others.
Enemies can *grab and use items* off the floor if they get to them first -- including bombs.
Picked up a *health charge* - walk over them to absorb them for instant health.
Your bag sorts items by category. Tap an item to use, equip, or inspect it, or assign it to an action slot.
Hard hits *knock creatures back*, including you - causing extra damage from hitting a wall or another creature.
The map shows where you've explored and how levels are linked. Activated *beacons* let you fast-travel.
You broke a *one-time door* - it shatters under you and disappears, letting creatures through.
Perks customise your build. Spend points on level-up via the *+ button*.
Firing at an *adjacent* target takes a *-50 accuracy penalty*. Get some distance, or use melee weapons.
Stairs lead to lower levels.  You can usally go back up again.  Check the *map* to see where you are.
If creatures don't have time to react, attacks always hit and do extra damage.  *Doors*, *smoke*, *invisibility*, and speed all help get surprise attacks.
No solid ground beside your foe to charge onto.
{player} snatches the {item} out of the air!
The {attacker} hits {player} for {damage} damage, knocking {player} back {kb}.
The {attacker} hits the {target} for {damage} damage, knocking the {target} back {kb}.
{player} hits the {target} for {damage} damage, knocking the {target} back {kb}.
The {item} fizzles because it lacks charge.
The {mob} is shoved into a wall for {damage} damage by {origin}.
The {mob} slams into the {into}, taking {damage} damage.
The {mob} slams into the {into}, taking {damage} damage and knocking the {into} back {kb}.
The {mob} slams into a wall, taking {damage} damage.
Path interrupted: a {mob} comes into view.
A very rare exotic gem of polished void-glass, prized at the hearth.
A very rare exotic gem that pulses with crimson life.
A rare metal gem, warm and ruddy; a sturdier forge reagent.
A very rare exotic gem glowing electric cyan.
A rare metal gem; the most prized of the forge's ingots.
A common violet keystone attuned to crystal.
A common clear keystone attuned to the shining halls.
A common green keystone attuned to stone; the hearth's workhorse reagent.
A very rare exotic gem veined with green fire.
A common ember-red keystone attuned to the gothic depths.
A rare metal gem, cool and bright; a sturdier forge reagent.
Ranged enemies such as *imps* can be a pain.  There are a lot of ways to deal with them.
You could close the distance with them, use something like *smoke* to prevent them from shooting, or *grapple* them and pull them close.
Or you could trick them into shooting *their own allies*?
The common orange *cat* is a frequent visitor to the dungeon.
Legend has it that these creatures can be powerful allies if treated well.
Cats sometimes bring their *kittens* into the dungeon.
Leveling up is done by collecting *orbs*, not by killing enemies for xp
Armor reduces incoming *physical* damage by a roll on each hit.
But *AP (armor-piercing) attacks bypass armor entirely* - wraiths and some weapons deal it.
Check a foe in the *Look* window to see whether your armor will help.
*Wands* fire ranged effects and run on *charges*.
A *charge pill* refills one charge on every wand you carry.
The *Wandmaster* perk raises the power of every wand.
*Beacons* are fast-travel points. Step beside one to light it.
Once lit, hop between activated beacons from the *map*.
A *teleport orb* throws you to any visible tile.
*Bombs* are thrown for area effects - fire, blast, smoke, freeze and more.
Only *thrown weapons* (like throwing knives) and consumables can be thrown - not your gear.
The *Bomb Jack* perk makes every bomb you throw stronger.
A slammed foe takes *extra damage* if it hits a wall or another mob.
The *Knockback* perk adds push to your melee swings.
Most enemies belong to a *faction* and won't attack their own kind.
Some factions are *enemies of each other* - red and black ants will fight.
Lure rivals together and let them thin each other out.
Hitting a foe that *can't see you* is a *surprise attack* - 1.5x damage.
Stay out of sight, invisible, or hidden to set one up.
The *Stealth* perk shrinks how far enemies notice you.
A blue, shield shaped gem in a gold setting.
Amulet that *boosts your armor stat*.\nStacks with worn armor.
An intricate sign traced in silver, it seems to aid your precision in combat.
A pale gem set in silver, it shines brightly.
Light can change how creatures and plants act.
Amulet that *radiates light around you*.\nReveals tiles + repels light-hating mobs.
A complex magical token that helps you move faster.
Amulet that *boosts evasion*.\nFewer enemy hits land.
A grey, unremarkable amulet to look at.
Protects from magical and energy attacks.
Amulet that *boosts magic resistance*.\nReduces incoming magic damage.
Ants make this ultra nourishing jelly, as healthy as it is revolting.
A nutritious ant jelly. *Eat to satisfy hunger*.\nMid-tier food.
A heavy iron sphere full of magical powder.
Damages and pushes away everything around the point of impact.
A bigger explosive. *Wider blast, more damage*.\nClear groups.
A very lifelike small enamel frog -- or is it a real frog staying very still?
Use it from a quickslot to jump rapidly away from or toward your foes.
A stone that *teleports you a few squares* on activation.\nShorter range than a teleport orb.
This nasty, underhanded weapon is permanently coated in poison.
Creates poison clouds on the target.
A cursed blade that *bleeds enemies on hit*.\nDamage-over-time stacks.
A lacquered ball with a spring-loaded brass hinge and a hungry little click.
Throw it at a creature to trap it inside. Throw a full one to release its occupant.
Throw to catch a creature in the ball.\n*Stores it* for later release. Pet acquisition.
It protects you somewhat against physical attacks.
Heavier chain armor. *Stronger damage reduction* than scale.\nDoesn't block AP.
A scrap of magical energy for your items.
Walk over to refill *one charge* on your equipped wand.\nUse before crucial spells.
Punchy blast bomb that *knocks back* - line a foe up with a wall for bonus damage.
A heavy, brutal sword that can block a good deal of damage
Hits hard, knocks back, protects  a bit -- nice.
A solid mid-weight sword.\nBalanced damage and accuracy.
A short bladed dagger, a classic weapon.
Not especially deadly but strikes rapidly.
A simple light blade. *Fast strikes, modest damage*.\nGood for early game.
apply a magical effect to the target area
create an explosion at the target
ignite a fireball at the target
chill and delay the target area
coax mushrooms up from the target
shock the target and nearby creatures
strike a single target for direct damage
spread a layer of oil across the target area
release a poison cloud at the target
reshape the target area - rerolls floor tiles and transforms nearby creatures into similarly-sized kin
recharge every wand in your bag
release a smoke cloud at the target
tear a void at the target - pulls nearby creatures in and crumbles the ground
create a pool of water at the target
On impact, deals damage and ignites tiles around the target.
Throw to ignite a tile and any mob on it. Fire burns over time and *spreads*.
So tasty that a stray cat or dog you throw it to will follow you for the rest of its life.
A fresh fish. *Eat to satisfy hunger*.\nMidweight food.
Glows on the floor with an attention-catching twinkle.
A rotting, disgusting gob of mystery meat.
There are probably particularly vile creatures that would be glad to be given this.
Rotten meat. *Edible but unpleasant*.\nUse only when starving.
A frosted vial that shatters into a numbing burst.
Chills enemies for a long time, delays their next action, and extinguishes flames.
Throw to chill nearby mobs.\nFrozen mobs *can't act* for a few turns - ideal for escapes.
A very lifelike small enamel frog -- or is it a real frog staying very still?
Use it from a quickslot to jump rapidly away from or toward your foes.
A jump-tool. *Activate to leap several squares*.\nIgnores intervening tiles.
A multicolored enamel frog -- or is it a real frog staying very still?
Use it from a quickslot to jump rapidly away from or toward your foes.
A hook on a strong rope.
Can pull enemies -- smaller ones, at least -- toward you.
Throw at a tile to *pull yourself there*.\nIgnores chasms - emergency escape.
It doesn't work instantly, it helps you regenerate over time.
Drink to start regenerating HP over a few turns.\n*Save one for emergencies.*
Walk over to absorb +HP instantly.\n*Powerups are free pickups* - they don't enter your bag.
Thick plates of steel encase your body.
Lots of protection against physical attack, at the cost of speed.
Heaviest body armor. *Top damage reduction*.\nDoesn't block AP attacks.
Ipwergis-pudding is a magically delectable food with many powers.
Grants insights if eaten; magical creatures might be very pleased to be given it.
A strange creature.\nMystery item - try using it.
A small jade bull, head lowered, hooves churning. The stone is warm to the touch.
Use to dash to a visible foe, strike them, and send them tumbling back.
Activate to *charge a target*, hitting with knockback. Great for breaking up groups.
A jade crab carved in perfect detail, its claws raised in a warding gesture.
Invoke to wrap yourself in a momentary shell of jade light.
Throw at a kitten to *tame it as a pet*.\nPets fight enemies that turn on you.
A peaceful green fish carved from jade, cool and smooth in the hand.
Hold the fish and you pass through the world like a blur.
Throw at a mouse to *tame it as a pet*.\nPets follow you and fight alongside you.
A long, sharp blade. *High damage*.\nSlightly slower than the dagger.
A sturdy kite shield offering good protection.
Larger offhand shield with *more armor*.\nReplaces a weapon's off-hand slot.
A frost-bound throwing blade that chills whatever it strikes.
Throw for ranged damage that also *chills* the target - slowing it
column3: and freezing it if it's wet.

en: A heavy throwing blade that hits far harder than a knife.
Throw for heavy ranged damage - the strongest thrown blade.
A glass sphere of dark oil. Shatters and slicks the floor on impact.
Throw to coat a tile in oil.\nOiled mobs are *vulnerable to fire* - throw a fire bomb to ignite.
A surprisingly common and nutritious food.
A simple pear. *Eat to satisfy hunger*.\nBasic food.
A long elegant pear, reportedly the kind eaten at high meetings.
A fine pear. *Eat to satisfy hunger*, with a small bonus.\nUpgraded food.
A specially ripe pear, dripping with juice. Five times more filling than the ordinary kind.
A delicious pear. *Eat to satisfy hunger*, with a bigger bonus.\nGreat food.
A pear with skin that shimmers like polished silver. Mysterious.
A magical pear. *Eat to satisfy hunger + gain a magical boost*.\nRare.
A fizzy potion that seems to weigh nothing.
Drink to *sense every mob* on the level for a short time.\nScouting tool.
The drinker becomes briefly ethereal and can pass through walls.
Drink to *pass through walls* briefly.\nGreat for escaping a corner.
A clear potion that crackles faintly.
Reveals the layout of the entire level on a draught.
Drink to *reveal the level layout*.\nMap-uncover effect.
A clear potion that hides the imbiber from sight for a short while.
Drink to *become invisible*.\n+20 evasion; can't be targeted by ranged. Ends on attack.
A glutinous potion that seems made of eyeballs.
Allows the drinker to percieve distant creatures.
Drink to *levitate*.\nIgnores ground hazards and pits.
A sickly green elixir. Vile to drink, devastating to throw.
If thrown, creates a puff of poison vapor.
Throw to *poison* a target.\nDon't drink - it'll hurt YOU.
A glittering brew that infuses the drinker with magical energy.
Increases the power of wands while its effects are active.
Drink to *empower your next spell or potion*.\nSave for a big wand zap.
A sphere of power that makes you stronger.
A powerup orb. *Grants a perk point*.\nSpend it on the character screen.
A fine dagger capable of slicing a foe to ribbons.
Often manages to get right past a foe's armor plates.
A close-in punching blade. *Modest melee with bonus AP*.\nAP bypasses armor.
Rapiers do moderate damage but you can attack more often with them.
A precision blade. *Good accuracy and reach*.\nReliable melee weapon.
Rapiers do moderate damage but you can attack more often with them.
Overlapping iron scales on a leather backing.
It protects you fairly well against physical attacks.
Armor cuts physical damage, but *AP attacks bypass it* - check enemy stats.
Armor cuts physical damage, but *AP attacks bypass it* - check enemy stats.
Protects a bit against physical damage.
Offhand shield. *Adds armor + evasion*.\nReplaces a weapon's off-hand slot.
A ball of tightly coiled wire that can release powerful current.
Lighting bolts arc from foe to foe, doing extra damage to wet targets.
Throw to *chain lightning* between nearby mobs.\nWet enemies take double damage.
A razor-edged throwing star - twice the bite of a knife.
Throw for ranged damage - hits harder than a throwing knife.
A black glass bomb packed with smoldering chemicals.
Shatters into a thick cloud of sight-blocking smoke.
Throw to drop a smoke cloud.\nSmoke *blocks line of sight* - great for breaking ranged attacks.
A plain iron blade, friend of many an adventurer.
As well as decent damage, it's heavy enough to knock smaller enemies back a bit.
A solid melee weapon. Its *plusses* scale with your character level.
A plump pink chunk of tasty meat.
Quality meat. *Eat to satisfy hunger*.\nGood food.
A swirling crystal sphere. Use to open the world map and teleport to any active beacon. Throw to scatter every caught creature across the dungeon.
Throw at any visible tile to teleport there - escape, grab loot, or reposition.
Bounces back to the throwers feet after striking.
Throwing this at a {mobs} tames it.
This well balanced knife can be thrown from some way off.
Minor magic helps it return to its thrower -- a bit.
Throw at a target for ranged damage.\n*One-shot* per knife - bring stacks.
A blazing torch that casts bright light all around.
Lights up the area, potentially changing how creatures and plants behave.  Also helps you go a bit faster.
A torch. *Lights up dark tiles around you*.\nCounters light-hating mobs.
Aim and {verb} at a visible foe to dash to them and strike.
Aim and {verb} to yank the contents of the target tile to your side.
Aim and {verb} to leap to a chosen tile within range.
Aim and {verb} to summon a {mob} at your side.
This powerful magical vine wraps itself around a foe, allowing you to haul them nearer.
Can pull enemies -- even quite large ones -- toward you.
Like a grappling hook, but *vines pull enemies to you* instead of pulling you to them.
A powerful bomb that draws in matter, creating a void.
Destroys floors and pulls creatures toward it.
Throw to open a *void zone* that displaces mobs.\nEmergency crowd control.
A wand bound in tarnished silver and tipped with flickering crystals.
This specialized wand can instantly banish creatures that are not really real.
Fires a *banishment ray*. *Banishable* mobs are instantly defeated.\nWorks on most undead.
A blunt brass wand. Concussive damage in a small area.
Fires a *circular blast* at the target tile.\nDamages all mobs in radius.
A blackened iron wand inlaid with red runes. Detonates the target tile in a ball of fire.
Blows the target location to smithereens.
Fires a *larger area explosion + knockback*.\nClear groups.
A wand decorated with a small animal head.
Summons a loyal dog to protect you.
Summons a *temporary dog ally*.\nPet helps you fight.
A blackened wand crackling with embers. Ignites the target.
Spreads fire around the target point
Fires a fire bolt that *ignites* the target tile.\nLimited charges - manage uses carefully.
Causes mushrooms to grow at the target area.
Plants *fungus tiles* that release spores.\nArea-denial tool.
A green wand bound with vine.
Engenders vegetation at the target point.
Plants *grass tiles* that obscure vision.\nUse for cover.
A wand that crackles with energy.
Effect jumps from target to target and is stronger in water -- beware.
Fires a *chain lightning bolt*.\nJumps between mobs. Wet mobs take 2x damage.
A short wand topped with a pale chip of crystal.
Sends out a magical bolt that does moderate damage.
Fires a homing magic missile dealing direct damage.\n*Always hits* once aimed - no roll-to-hit.
A dark wand slick to the touch.
Creates a layer of oil at the target location.
Pours *oil on a tile + mobs*.\nIgnite for big fire damage.
A wand that constantly shifts and changes.
Can turn a creature into something else.
Transforms the target into a *random weaker creature*.\nReally useful against tough enemies.
A pale blue wand that drips with water.
Creates a pool of water at the target location.
Sprays water across tiles.\nMakes mobs *wet* (2x lightning damage) and extinguishes fire.
A glass vial of magically compressed water.
Gushes water everywhere, washing smaller creatures back.
Throw to *soak a tile + mobs in water*.\nWet mobs take 2x lightning damage.
A fragment of intrinsic power to add to yourself.
Walk over to gain *experience* toward your next level.\nFree pickup, no slot used.
Suddenly appears at the side of an enemy{cooldown}
Phases aside to avoid an incoming attack{cooldown}
These horrible phantoms seem to have something to do with the beacon system.  They are huge in size and malice.
High evasion, armor piercing, magic resistant -- good luck.
A fierce barbarian princess, pursuing her own unknowable goals
She's peaceful, but perhaps an enemy could be tricked into angering her?
A large, menacing bat. It flutters around in the shadows, its eyes glowing with malevolent intent.
Not very strong, but it can cross chasms.
A proud and free ant worker, far superior to its craven and robotic Red Ant foes.
Not that strong by itself, and often targets the Red Ant scum rather than you.
A colony of black ants has broken through into the dungeon here, intent on extracting resources.
It'll just keep on spawning ants, so destroy it fast.
A small, skittering mouse with a fiery disposition.
You won't want to kill this creature close up.
A large, unpleasant creature. It oozes across the floor, consuming everything in its path.  It doesn't seem to belong here, but it does seem hungry.
Blobs are slow and tough.  They'll fight anything, not just you.
The Prodigy might once have been a soldier bug, but it has evolved into something disturbingly humanoid.
Strong melee, good armor, consider magic attacks.
A sleek and agile cat, always on the prowl for its next meal.  Mice and rats beware.
Cats hunt mice and rats but are *not hostile to you* unless attacked.  Fond of fish.
A friendly white cat that seems a long way from home; it is an expert at catching mice.
A tough foe if you provoke it.
An imp of unusual size, hiding itself behind a leering mask.
Ranged magical attacks tend to ignore your armor.
A loyal and fierce dog, trained to hunt and protect.  Who's a good boy?  This dog is.
Perhaps you could throw it some meat.
A rival wand-user who fights alongside a shielding Jade Crab. Best dealt with before he can fling spells.
A rival adventurer with the elusive Jade Fish, striking from stealth with bombs and blades.
A rival warrior who charges in behind his Jade Bull with powerful blows.
This otherwordly being is vulnerable to being Banished.
By devouring corpses, this creature can spawn further beings.
On death, this creature explodes in fire.
This creature is immune to fire.
This creatures hits ignite tiles around the target.
Feasting on mushrooms causes this creature to breed explosively.
This creature will not pick up items.
This creature does not frighten easily.
This creature is immune to poison.
This creatures venomous attacks poison the foe.
This terrifying being frightens nearby susceptible creatures.
Can send out servants to do its bidding.
A twisted piece of cloth that adopts a malevolent form, ethereal yet all too real.
Far, far bigger than imps are supposed to be, you dread to imagine what might be hidden beneath that ornate mask.
Won't close with you -- it'll just keep damaging you with magic from afar.
A towering, nightmarish incarnation of sadness and hunger.  Stories say that if you try to flee it will simply appear again at your side.
Causes terror in its enemies and can teleport.
A large, revolting, indiscriminately predatory creature, ravenous for any and all flesh.  When gorged, it vomits up further monstrosities.
The more weak creatures it devours, the more horrible blobs it can produce.
A small kitten that looks up to its parent cat.  You can only hope it survives long enough to grow up.
Follows its mother cat about.  Not a threat.
A heavyset, scaly humanoid hefting a battered meat-cleaver. The blade looks plenty capable of finding the gaps in plate.
A small, scaly humanoid wielding a crude blade. It hisses at you with malice.
Weak by itself -- not weak when with lots of other kobolds.
A tall, scaly humanoid in worn ceremonial plate. He barks orders to his kin and raises a glowing standard.
The general *heals and hastes* its kobold subordinates.  Kill it first.
A scaly humanoid clutching a bundle of crude spears. It hangs back from the fight, looking for a clear throw.
Physical ranged attacker -- a problem when teamed up with other kobolds.
An imp that hides its identity behind a grotesque mask.  It's slightly larger and stronger than its smaller kin.
Close with it, or else out-damage it from a distance.
A powerful, enigmatic wraith trapped somehow in the physical world.  Not clear whether it wants to be here.  Clear that it doesn't want *you* to be here.
High evasion, high armor piercing, resists magic.
A loathsome bug. This cockroach-like creature has few redeeming features.  It's not very strong.
What twisted this huge dog into such a malevolent and brutal form?  It's slavering jaws seem to fill the room ahead.
Fast and strong; try to get distance from it.
A small, hostile creature hiding behind a rudimentary mask.
It's weak close up, so get near it.
A small, skittering mouse. It's timid and tends to flee from you.  Loves mushrooms and can breed explosively when mushrooms are available.
Harmless alone, but if you hurt one you enrage them all.
This is not necessarily a hostile creature.
This well-armed and disciplined creature doesn't seem native to the dungeon.  What threats and lies drew him here?
Tough armor, heavy hitter, stay back.
A stunted orc whose misshapen body can contain only evil.
Orc presidents fire from range and pack a punch.\nClose the distance fast or use cover.
Armed with a heavy crossbow, this disciplined and grim orc soldier would rather be somewhere else.
An infamous, omnicidal feline horror from the Whispering Mountain that terrifies all around it and moves like psychopathic lightning.
Hits like a truck -- don't get in melee without serious physical protection.
A wand user who relies on magic skill and his shielding Jade Crab to survive.
Holder of the elusive Jade Fish, she uses bombs, stealth and keen senses.
The Warrior charges ahead with his Jade Bull and powerful attacks.
This creature has a ranged attack with damage {range}
A mangy rat with too many teeth. Slightly tougher than a roach but no scarier.
This isn't a very strong enemy, even though they tend to come in groups.
A proud and free ant worker, far superior to its craven and robotic Black Ant foes.
Not that strong by itself, and often targets the Black Ant scum rather than you.
A colony of red ants has broken through into the dungeon here, intent on extracting resources.
It'll just keep on spawning ants, so destroy it fast.
Sees for {vision} tiles, may wake if foe within {wake} tiles.
This creature is huge in size.
This soldier bug has a glossy carapace and razor-sharp pincers.  Due to tactical training it's far more robust than other insects this size.
Heavy armor; perhaps magic or armor piercing weapons would be good.
This creature can attack rather quickly.
This creatures attacks are rather slow.
A small, skittering spider. It looks poisonous.  It's certainly fast moving, and it seems to regard you as edible.
Closes with you and deals *poison*.  Get rid of it before it reaches you, maybe.
Far nastier than its ordinary doombat brethren, it wants to *suck your blood*.  But it'll settle for just swarming you until you die.
Like a regular bat but tougher and more likely to be carrying something useful.
A bug that evolved into a humanoid -- but something went unpleasantly wrong.  Limbs stick out in all directions.  Most of them look sharp.
Strong and well armored in melee.
A particularly cruel imp, this one capers and leers maliciously while still hiding behind its unpleasant mask.
Beware of group ranged damage.  Smoke or vegetation may be your friend.
A twisted piece of cloth whose mouth opens wider... and wider...
Hard to hit, but at least its retainers are vulnerable to being dispelled.
This ghastly thing must once have been an imp -- but it's grown to insane proportions and moves with terrifying speed.
Extreme ranged damage -- be hard to hit, or close with it.
This enraged and bitter kobold is a lot stronger, a lot crazier, and a lot more dangerous than the rest.
Extremely high melee damage -- consider avoiding it.
This kobold is a lot more manly than the others, and seems to have six pack abs.  Is... this... Sparta??
Tough in melee, and its supporters will wear you down.
A kobold ruler, decked in gold and jewels. This is the most powerful and respected a kobold can ever be.
The monarch *heals and hastes its followers*.  Kill it first or the whole pack outlasts you.
Somehow, this otherwise harmless kobold has got hold of a crude firearm, which it uses to dominate its kin.
Heavy ranged damage -- better to make yourself hard to hit.
An imp that hides its identity behind a grotesque mask.  This one has grown really quite large and seems more independent.
Best to close with it fast before it can fire too many ranged attacks.
This cockroach like creature seethes with a malevolent intelligence.
Like a regular roach but tougher.
A small, hostile creature hiding behind a rudimentary mask.  This particular one seems to be a bit bolder than the others.
Beware of getting hit by multiple ranged attacks.
This rat sees itself as a king among rats, but it's just as mangy and disease-ridden as the others.
Like a regular rat but tougher.
A highly experienced insect, veteran of many tough fights.  It has smeared camo paint on its thick carapace.
A soldier bug with *heavy armor*, it's hard to kill with weaker weapons.
A revolting spider laden with venom.  This one seems to be particularly unpleasant and leggy.
Letting this spider and its daughters get close enough to poison you would be bad.
A prowling wolf, long since adapted to life in the dungeon, looking for flesh.
Wolves are quick and *bite hard*.  Don't let them surround you.
A faint phantom shape that seems to come from the world beyond the Beacons. Its outline wavers but its hostility is obvious.
Wraiths ignore most armor and are hard to hit.  Try area-of-effect or banishment.
Bomb damage is halved per level; bomb effects are reduced; increasing chance to catch incoming bombs.
Good for using bombs at close range -- or for dealing with bomb-carrying enemies.
Essential skill for those who rely on bombs.
An extra 2 throw range per level; throw action cost x0.85 per level.
Powerful synergy with bombs and thrown weapons -- and perhaps with fish, too.
Adds magical extra distance to abilities that move you -- such as the Frog, the Jade Bull, and the Blinkstone.
Grants a phase-dodge: when about to be hit
column3: you blink to an adjacent square and avoid the whole attack. Cooldown shortens per level (11 - level turns).

en: Lifesaving against hard hitters - but it needs a free square to blink to, and it recharges between dodges.
See further through clouds or plants per level. Distant smoke and trees will still hide what's behind them.
A good way of getting surprise attacks, or of making smoke bombs and vegetation count.
On kill: stacking KILLER buff (x0.85 attack/move cost per stack). Each kill adds more stacks and longer duration.
Useful when fighting a lot of enemies -- if you can build up momentum.
Levels 1-5: +1 tile of melee knockback per level (max 5 extra tiles). Levels 6-10: each additional level adds extra damage when the knocked-back target strikes a solid object.
Useful it enemies are in a line or have their backs to a wall, because knockback can do damage each time an enemy slams into something.
Half the time you consume food or a potion
column3: it restores level/2 charges to every wand and item you carry.

en: Extremely useful for heavy wand users, or for using items that need a lot of charges per use.
Enemies are less likely to wake up, and can see you from less far away, for each level of Stealth.
Makes it harder for sleeping enemies to notice you -- good for surprise attacks and staying out of trouble.
Wands gain power for every level of this perk, giving them more damage, more lasting effects, and more charge capacity.
Every wand gets *+1 effective level* per perk level; vital for wand users.
Non-flying creatures fall through to the level below.
Flammable - catches fire from heat sources.
A stone altar.  It's meaning is unclear but sinister.
An active teleport beacon. From here you can get to any other active teleport beacon.
A dormant teleport beacon. Walk adjacent to ignite it - then you can teleport to it from any other active beacon.
A deep, dark chasm. Falling carries you to a deeper level, in the event that you survive the fall.
A closed crystal door.  These magical doors open only for you, not for hostile creatures.
A closed wooden door. Walk into it to push it open.  All creatures can pass through these, either by opening them or by creeping through gaps.
An open doorway. Walk through freely.
Plain stone floor. Convenient for walking on.
A walkway; works like any ordinary floor.
A burning lamp. Lights the surrounding tiles.
A fragile crystal barrier that only you can break, put here in an attempt to keep the creatures out; once gone, it never returns.
Stairs leading down. Step onto them and descend.
Stairs leading up. Step onto them and ascend.
Solid stone. Blocks all movement and sight.
Teleport to level {depth}? (1 orb, you have {orbs})
This removes the saved game in slot {slot}.
This save could not be loaded. It may be corrupt or from an incompatible version.
Saved by {saved}; this game is {current}. It may not load correctly.
Erase tip history (show all tips again)
Grant a brand to a selected equippable item.
Grant +1 level to a selected equippable item.
Grant ESP and Insight at maximum stacks and fill every eligible square with smoke.
Open the map and travel to a chosen beacon; with no beacons
column3: teleport to a random spot at the shallowest depth.

en: Grant 8 stacks of Eightfold Defence: heavy armor and magic resist per level
column3: and immunity to negative status effects.

column4: and gain enough experience to reach the next level.

en: Create two sets of five bombs
column3: as if found two levels deeper.

en: Freeze every visible enemy at the maximum number of stacks.

column3: leaving a healing or mana pill on each affected square.

en: Turn every regular door on the level into a one-time door.

column3: as if found two levels deeper.

en: Create two random tools (never jade items).
Destroy all visible blood and deal heavy physical damage to every visible enemy
Choose any point on the level
column4: and erupt a great explosion there.

en: Set every square beside a statue or lamp ablaze
Summon eight autonomous clones of yourself on adjacent tiles.
Turn every regular door on the level into a crystal door.
Drive every visible enemy to turn hostile against all creatures.

as if found ten levels deeper.
